    29→// Evaluator provides the necessary contextual data for evaluating expressions
    30→// for a particular walk operation.
    31→type Evaluator struct {
    32→	// Operation defines what type of operation this evaluator is being used
    33→	// for.
    34→	Operation walkOperation
    35→
    36→	// Meta is contextual metadata about the current operation.
    37→	Meta *ContextMeta
    38→
    39→	// Config is the root node in the configuration tree.
    40→	Config *configs.Config
    41→
    42→	// Instances tracks the dynamic instances that are associated with each
    43→	// module call or resource. The graph walk gradually registers the
    44→	// set of instances for each object within the graph nodes for those
    45→	// objects, and so as long as the graph has been built correctly the
    46→	// set of instances for an object should always be available by the time
    47→	// we're evaluating expressions that refer to it.
    48→	Instances *instances.Expander
    49→
    50→	// NamedValues is where we keep the values of already-evaluated input
    51→	// variables, local values, and output values.
    52→	NamedValues *namedvals.State
    53→
    54→	// Plugins is the library of available plugin components (providers and
    55→	// provisioners) that we have available to help us evaluate expressions
    56→	// that interact with plugin-provided objects.
    57→	//
    58→	// From this we only access the schemas of the plugins, and don't otherwise
    59→	// interact with plugin instances.
    60→	Plugins *contextPlugins
    61→
    62→	// State is the current state, embedded in a wrapper that ensures that
    63→	// it can be safely accessed and modified concurrently.
    64→	State *states.SyncState
    65→
    66→	// Changes is the set of proposed changes, embedded in a wrapper that
    67→	// ensures they can be safely accessed and modified concurrently.
    68→	Changes *plans.ChangesSync
    69→
    70→	PlanTimestamp time.Time
    71→}
    72→
    73→// Scope creates an evaluation scope for the given module path and optional
    74→// resource.
    75→//
    76→// If the "self" argument is nil then the "self" object is not available
    77→// in evaluated expressions. Otherwise, it behaves as an alias for the given
    78→// address.
    79→func (e *Evaluator) Scope(data lang.Data, self addrs.Referenceable, source addrs.Referenceable, extFuncs lang.ExternalFuncs) *lang.Scope {
    80→	return &lang.Scope{
    81→		Data:          data,
    82→		ParseRef:      addrs.ParseRef,
    83→		SelfAddr:      self,
    84→		SourceAddr:    source,
    85→		PureOnly:      e.Operation != walkApply && e.Operation != walkDestroy && e.Operation != walkEval,
    86→		BaseDir:       ".", // Always current working directory for now.
    87→		PlanTimestamp: e.PlanTimestamp,
    88→		ExternalFuncs: extFuncs,
    89→	}
    90→}
    91→
    92→// evaluationStateData is an implementation of lang.Data that resolves
    93→// references primarily (but not exclusively) using information from a State.
    94→type evaluationStateData struct {
    95→	Evaluator *Evaluator
    96→
    97→	// ModulePath is the path through the dynamic module tree to the module
    98→	// that references will be resolved relative to.
    99→	ModulePath addrs.ModuleInstance
   100→
   101→	// InstanceKeyData describes the values, if any, that are accessible due
   102→	// to repetition of a containing object using "count" or "for_each"
   103→	// arguments. (It is _not_ used for the for_each inside "dynamic" blocks,
   104→	// since the user specifies in that case which variable name to locally
   105→	// shadow.)
   106→	InstanceKeyData InstanceKeyEvalData
   107→
   108→	// Operation records the type of walk the evaluationStateData is being used
   109→	// for.
   110→	Operation walkOperation
   111→}
   112→
   113→// InstanceKeyEvalData is the old name for instances.RepetitionData, aliased
   114→// here for compatibility. In new code, use instances.RepetitionData instead.
   115→type InstanceKeyEvalData = instances.RepetitionData
   116→
   117→// EvalDataForInstanceKey constructs a suitable InstanceKeyEvalData for
   118→// evaluating in a context that has the given instance key.
   119→//
   120→// The forEachMap argument can be nil when preparing for evaluation
   121→// in a context where each.value is prohibited, such as a destroy-time
   122→// provisioner. In that case, the returned EachValue will always be
   123→// cty.NilVal.
   124→func EvalDataForInstanceKey(key addrs.InstanceKey, forEachMap map[string]cty.Value) InstanceKeyEvalData {
   125→	var evalData InstanceKeyEvalData
   126→	if key == nil {
   127→		return evalData
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	keyValue := key.Value()
   131→	switch keyValue.Type() {
   132→	case cty.String:
   133→		evalData.EachKey = keyValue
   134→		evalData.EachValue = forEachMap[keyValue.AsString()]
   135→	case cty.Number:
   136→		evalData.CountIndex = keyValue
   137→	}
   138→	return evalData
   139→}
   140→
   141→// EvalDataForNoInstanceKey is a value of InstanceKeyData that sets no instance
   142→// key values at all, suitable for use in contexts where no keyed instance
   143→// is relevant.
   144→var EvalDataForNoInstanceKey = InstanceKeyEvalData{}
   145→
   146→// evaluationStateData must implement lang.Data
   147→var _ lang.Data = (*evaluationStateData)(nil)
   148→
   149→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetCountAttr(addr addrs.CountAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   150→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   151→	switch addr.Name {
   152→
   153→	case "index":
   154→		idxVal := d.InstanceKeyData.CountIndex
   155→		if idxVal == cty.NilVal {
   156→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   157→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   158→				Summary:  `Reference to "count" in non-counted context`,
   159→				Detail:   `The "count" object can only be used in "module", "resource", and "data" blocks, and only when the "count" argument is set.`,
   160→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   161→			})
   162→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Number), diags
   163→		}
   164→		return idxVal, diags
   165→
   166→	default:
   167→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   168→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   169→			Summary:  `Invalid "count" attribute`,
   170→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "count" object does not have an attribute named %q. The only supported attribute is count.index, which is the index of each instance of a resource block that has the "count" argument set.`, addr.Name),
   171→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   172→		})
   173→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   174→	}
   175→}
   176→
   177→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetForEachAttr(addr addrs.ForEachAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   178→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   179→	var returnVal cty.Value
   180→	switch addr.Name {
   181→
   182→	case "key":
   183→		returnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachKey
   184→	case "value":
   185→		returnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachValue
   186→
   187→		if returnVal == cty.NilVal {
   188→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   189→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   190→				Summary:  `each.value cannot be used in this context`,
   191→				Detail:   `A reference to "each.value" has been used in a context in which it is unavailable, such as when the configuration no longer contains the value in its "for_each" expression. Remove this reference to each.value in your configuration to work around this error.`,
   192→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   193→			})
   194→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags
   195→		}
   196→	default:
   197→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   198→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   199→			Summary:  `Invalid "each" attribute`,
   200→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "each" object does not have an attribute named %q. The supported attributes are each.key and each.value, the current key and value pair of the "for_each" attribute set.`, addr.Name),
   201→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   202→		})
   203→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	if returnVal == cty.NilVal {
   207→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   208→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   209→			Summary:  `Reference to "each" in context without for_each`,
   210→			Detail:   `The "each" object can be used only in "module" or "resource" blocks, and only when the "for_each" argument is set.`,
   211→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   212→		})
   213→		return cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags
   214→	}
   215→	return returnVal, diags
   216→}
   217→
   218→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetInputVariable(addr addrs.InputVariable, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   219→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   220→
   221→	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   222→	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   223→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendentForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   224→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   225→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   226→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   227→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("input variable read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   228→	}
   229→
   230→	config := moduleConfig.Module.Variables[addr.Name]
   231→	if config == nil {
   232→		var suggestions []string
   233→		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Variables {
   234→			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   235→		}
   236→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   237→		if suggestion != "" {
   238→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   239→		} else {
   240→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" This variable can be declared with a variable %q {} block.", addr.Name)
   241→		}
   242→
   243→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   244→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   245→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared input variable`,
   246→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An input variable with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   247→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   248→		})
   249→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   250→	}
   251→
   252→	// During the validate walk, input variables are always unknown so
   253→	// that we are validating the configuration for all possible input values
   254→	// rather than for a specific set. Checking against a specific set of
   255→	// input values then happens during the plan walk.
   256→	//
   257→	// This is important because otherwise the validation walk will tend to be
   258→	// overly strict, requiring expressions throughout the configuration to
   259→	// be complicated to accommodate all possible inputs, whereas returning
   260→	// unknown here allows for simpler patterns like using input values as
   261→	// guards to broadly enable/disable resources, avoid processing things
   262→	// that are disabled, etc. Terraform's static validation leans towards
   263→	// being liberal in what it accepts because the subsequent plan walk has
   264→	// more information available and so can be more conservative.
   265→	if d.Operation == walkValidate {
   266→		// Ensure variable sensitivity is captured in the validate walk
   267→		if config.Sensitive {
   268→			return cty.UnknownVal(config.Type).Mark(marks.Sensitive), diags
   269→		}
   270→		return cty.UnknownVal(config.Type), diags
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	val := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetInputVariableValue(d.ModulePath.InputVariable(addr.Name))
   274→
   275→	// Mark if sensitive
   276→	if config.Sensitive {
   277→		val = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   278→	}
   279→
   280→	return val, diags
   281→}
   282→
   283→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetLocalValue(addr addrs.LocalValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   284→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   285→
   286→	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   287→	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   288→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendentForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   289→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   290→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   291→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   292→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("local value read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   293→	}
   294→
   295→	config := moduleConfig.Module.Locals[addr.Name]
   296→	if config == nil {
   297→		var suggestions []string
   298→		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Locals {
   299→			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   300→		}
   301→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   302→		if suggestion != "" {
   303→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   304→		}
   305→
   306→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   307→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   308→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared local value`,
   309→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A local value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   310→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   311→		})
   312→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   313→	}
   314→
   315→	val := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetLocalValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   316→	return val, diags
   317→}
   318→
   319→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetModule(addr addrs.ModuleCall, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   320→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   321→
   322→	// Output results live in the module that declares them, which is one of
   323→	// the child module instances of our current module path.
   324→	moduleAddr := d.ModulePath.Module().Child(addr.Name)
   325→	absAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   326→
   327→	parentCfg := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendentForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   328→	callConfig, ok := parentCfg.Module.ModuleCalls[addr.Name]
   329→	if !ok {
   330→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   331→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   332→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared module`,
   333→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The configuration contains no %s.`, moduleAddr),
   334→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   335→		})
   336→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   337→	}
   338→
   339→	// We'll consult the configuration to see what output names we are
   340→	// expecting, so we can ensure the resulting object is of the expected
   341→	// type even if our data is incomplete for some reason.
   342→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.Descendent(moduleAddr)
   343→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   344→		// should never happen, since we have a valid module call above, this
   345→		// should be caught during static validation.
   346→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("output value read from %s, which has no configuration", moduleAddr))
   347→	}
   348→	outputConfigs := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs
   349→
   350→	// We don't do instance expansion during validation, and so we need to
   351→	// return an unknown value. Technically we should always return
   352→	// cty.DynamicVal here because the final value during plan will always
   353→	// be an object or tuple type with unpredictable attributes/elements,
   354→	// but because we never actually carry values forward from validation to
   355→	// planning we lie a little here and return unknown list and map types,
   356→	// just to give us more opportunities to catch author mistakes during
   357→	// validation.
   358→	//
   359→	// This means that in practice any expression that refers to a module
   360→	// call must be written to be valid for either a collection type or
   361→	// structural type of similar kind, so that it can be considered as
   362→	// valid during both the validate and plan walks.
   363→	if d.Operation == walkValidate {
   364→		atys := make(map[string]cty.Type, len(outputConfigs))
   365→		for name := range outputConfigs {
   366→			atys[name] = cty.DynamicPseudoType // output values are dynamically-typed
   367→		}
   368→		instTy := cty.Object(atys)
   369→
   370→		switch {
   371→		case callConfig.Count != nil:
   372→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.List(instTy)), diags
   373→		case callConfig.ForEach != nil:
   374→			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Map(instTy)), diags
   375→		default:
   376→			return cty.UnknownVal(instTy), diags
   377→		}
   378→	}
   379→
   380→	// For all other walk types, we proceed to dynamic evaluation of individual
   381→	// instances, using the global instance expander. An earlier graph node
   382→	// should always have registered the expansion of this module call before
   383→	// we get here, unless there's a bug in the graph builders.
   384→	allInstances := d.Evaluator.Instances
   385→	instKeyType, instKeys, known := allInstances.ExpandAbsModuleCall(absAddr)
   386→	if !known {
   387→		// If we don't know which instances exist then we can't really predict
   388→		// anything at all. We can't even predict the return type based on
   389→		// instKeyType because output values are dynamically-typed and so
   390→		// our final result will always be an object or tuple type whose
   391→		// attribute/element count we cannot predict.
   392→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   393→	}
   394→
   395→	instanceObjVal := func(instKey addrs.InstanceKey) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   396→		// This function must always return a valid value, even if it's
   397→		// just a cty.DynamicVal placeholder accompanying error diagnostics.
   398→		var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   399→
   400→		namedVals := d.Evaluator.NamedValues
   401→		moduleInstAddr := absAddr.Instance(instKey)
   402→		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(outputConfigs))
   403→		for name := range outputConfigs {
   404→			outputAddr := moduleInstAddr.OutputValue(name)
   405→
   406→			// Although we do typically expect the graph dependencies to
   407→			// ensure that values get registered before they are needed,
   408→			// we track depedencies with specific output values where
   409→			// possible, instead of with entire module calls, and so
   410→			// in this specific case it's valid for some of this call's
   411→			// output values to not be known yet, with the graph builder
   412→			// being responsible for making sure that no expression
   413→			// in the configuration can actually observe that.
   414→			if !namedVals.HasOutputValue(outputAddr) {
   415→				attrs[name] = cty.DynamicVal
   416→				continue
   417→			}
   418→			outputVal := namedVals.GetOutputValue(outputAddr)
   419→			attrs[name] = outputVal
   420→		}
   421→
   422→		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   423→	}
   424→
   425→	switch instKeyType {
   426→
   427→	case addrs.NoKeyType:
   428→		// In this case we should always have exactly one instance that
   429→		// is addrs.NoKey. If not then there's a bug in the [instances.Expander]
   430→		// implementation.
   431→		if len(instKeys) != 1 {
   432→			panic(fmt.Sprintf("module call has no instance key type but has %d instances (should be 1)", len(instKeys)))
   433→		}
   434→		ret, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKeys[0])
   435→		diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   436→		return ret, diags
   437→
   438→	case addrs.IntKeyType:
   439→		// We can assume that the instance keys are in ascending numerical order
   440→		// and are consecutive, per the contract of allInstances.ExpandModuleCall.
   441→		elems := make([]cty.Value, 0, len(instKeys))
   442→		for _, instKey := range instKeys {
   443→			instVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)
   444→			elems = append(elems, instVal)
   445→			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   446→		}
   447→		return cty.TupleVal(elems), diags
   448→
   449→	case addrs.StringKeyType:
   450→		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(instKeys))
   451→		for _, instKey := range instKeys {
   452→			instVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)
   453→			attrs[string(instKey.(addrs.StringKey))] = instVal
   454→			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   455→		}
   456→		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   457→
   458→	default:
   459→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   460→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   461→			Summary:  `Unsupported instance key type`,
   462→			Detail: fmt.Sprintf(
   463→				`Module call %s has instance key type %#v, which is not supported by the expression evaluator. This is a bug in Terraform.`,
   464→				absAddr, instKeyType,
   465→			),
   466→			Subject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   467→		})
   468→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   469→	}
   470→}
   471→
   472→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetPathAttr(addr addrs.PathAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   473→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   474→	switch addr.Name {
   475→
   476→	case "cwd":
   477→		var err error
   478→		var wd string
   479→		if d.Evaluator.Meta != nil {
   480→			// Meta is always non-nil in the normal case, but some test cases
   481→			// are not so realistic.
   482→			wd = d.Evaluator.Meta.OriginalWorkingDir
   483→		}
   484→		if wd == "" {
   485→			wd, err = os.Getwd()
   486→			if err != nil {
   487→				diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   488→					Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   489→					Summary:  `Failed to get working directory`,
   490→					Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The value for path.cwd cannot be determined due to a system error: %s`, err),
   491→					Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   492→				})
   493→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   494→			}
   495→		}
   496→		// The current working directory should always be absolute, whether we
   497→		// just looked it up or whether we were relying on ContextMeta's
   498→		// (possibly non-normalized) path.
   499→		wd, err = filepath.Abs(wd)
   500→		if err != nil {
   501→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   502→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   503→				Summary:  `Failed to get working directory`,
   504→				Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The value for path.cwd cannot be determined due to a system error: %s`, err),
   505→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   506→			})
   507→			return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   508→		}
   509→
   510→		return cty.StringVal(filepath.ToSlash(wd)), diags
   511→
   512→	case "module":
   513→		moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendentForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   514→		if moduleConfig == nil {
   515→			// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   516→			// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   517→			panic(fmt.Sprintf("module.path read from module %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   518→		}
   519→		sourceDir := moduleConfig.Module.SourceDir
   520→		return cty.StringVal(filepath.ToSlash(sourceDir)), diags
   521→
   522→	case "root":
   523→		sourceDir := d.Evaluator.Config.Module.SourceDir
   524→		return cty.StringVal(filepath.ToSlash(sourceDir)), diags
   525→
   526→	default:
   527→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, []string{"cwd", "module", "root"})
   528→		if suggestion != "" {
   529→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   530→		}
   531→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   532→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   533→			Summary:  `Invalid "path" attribute`,
   534→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "path" object does not have an attribute named %q.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   535→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   536→		})
   537→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   538→	}
   539→}
   540→
   541→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   542→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   543→	// First we'll consult the configuration to see if an resource of this
   544→	// name is declared at all.
   545→	moduleAddr := d.ModulePath
   546→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendentForInstance(moduleAddr)
   547→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   548→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   549→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   550→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("resource value read from %s, which has no configuration", moduleAddr))
   551→	}
   552→
   553→	config := moduleConfig.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr)
   554→	if config == nil {
   555→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   556→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   557→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared resource`,
   558→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A resource %q %q has not been declared in %s`, addr.Type, addr.Name, moduleDisplayAddr(moduleAddr)),
   559→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   560→		})
   561→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   562→	}
   563→
   564→	// Build the provider address from configuration, since we may not have
   565→	// state available in all cases.
   566→	// We need to build an abs provider address, but we can use a default
   567→	// instance since we're only interested in the schema.
   568→	schema := d.getResourceSchema(addr, config.Provider)
   569→	if schema == nil {
   570→		// This shouldn't happen, since validation before we get here should've
   571→		// taken care of it, but we'll show a reasonable error message anyway.
   572→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   573→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   574→			Summary:  `Missing resource type schema`,
   575→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("No schema is available for %s in %s. This is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.", addr, config.Provider),
   576→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   577→		})
   578→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   579→	}
   580→	ty := schema.ImpliedType()
   581→
   582→	rs := d.Evaluator.State.Resource(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   583→
   584→	if rs == nil {
   585→		switch d.Operation {
   586→		case walkPlan, walkApply:
   587→			// During plan and apply as we evaluate each removed instance they
   588→			// are removed from the working state. Since we know there are no
   589→			// instances, return an empty container of the expected type.
   590→			switch {
   591→			case config.Count != nil:
   592→				return cty.EmptyTupleVal, diags
   593→			case config.ForEach != nil:
   594→				return cty.EmptyObjectVal, diags
   595→			default:
   596→				// While we can reference an expanded resource with 0
   597→				// instances, we cannot reference instances that do not exist.
   598→				// Due to the fact that we may have direct references to
   599→				// instances that may end up in a root output during destroy
   600→				// (since a planned destroy cannot yet remove root outputs), we
   601→				// need to return a dynamic value here to allow evaluation to
   602→				// continue.
   603→				log.Printf("[ERROR] unknown instance %q referenced during %s", addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath), d.Operation)
   604→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   605→			}
   606→
   607→		case walkImport:
   608→			// Import does not yet plan resource changes, so new resources from
   609→			// config are not going to be found here. Once walkImport fully
   610→			// plans resources, this case should not longer be needed.
   611→			// In the single instance case, we can return a typed unknown value
   612→			// for the instance to better satisfy other expressions using the
   613→			// value. This of course will not help if statically known
   614→			// attributes are expected to be known elsewhere, but reduces the
   615→			// number of problematic configs for now.
   616→			// Unlike in plan and apply above we can't be sure the count or
   617→			// for_each instances are empty, so we return a DynamicVal. We
   618→			// don't really have a good value to return otherwise -- empty
   619→			// values will fail for direct index expressions, and unknown
   620→			// Lists and Maps could fail in some type unifications.
   621→			switch {
   622→			case config.Count != nil:
   623→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   624→			case config.ForEach != nil:
   625→				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   626→			default:
   627→				return cty.UnknownVal(ty), diags
   628→			}
   629→
   630→		default:
   631→			// We should only end up here during the validate walk,
   632→			// since later walks should have at least partial states populated
   633→			// for all resources in the configuration.
   634→			return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   635→		}
   636→	}
   637→
   638→	// Decode all instances in the current state
   639→	instances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}
   640→	pendingDestroy := d.Operation == walkDestroy
   641→	for key, is := range rs.Instances {
   642→		if is == nil || is.Current == nil {
   643→			// Assume we're dealing with an instance that hasn't been created yet.
   644→			instances[key] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   645→			continue
   646→		}
   647→
   648→		instAddr := addr.Instance(key).Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   649→
   650→		change := d.Evaluator.Changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(instAddr, addrs.NotDeposed)
   651→		if change != nil {
   652→			// Don't take any resources that are yet to be deleted into account.
   653→			// If the referenced resource is CreateBeforeDestroy, then orphaned
   654→			// instances will be in the state, as they are not destroyed until
   655→			// after their dependants are updated.
   656→			if change.Action == plans.Delete {
   657→				if !pendingDestroy {
   658→					continue
   659→				}
   660→			}
   661→		}
   662→
   663→		// Planned resources are temporarily stored in state with empty values,
   664→		// and need to be replaced by the planned value here.
   665→		if is.Current.Status == states.ObjectPlanned {
   666→			if change == nil {
   667→				// If the object is in planned status then we should not get
   668→				// here, since we should have found a pending value in the plan
   669→				// above instead.
   670→				diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   671→					Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   672→					Summary:  "Missing pending object in plan",
   673→					Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("Instance %s is marked as having a change pending but that change is not recorded in the plan. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it.", instAddr),
   674→					Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   675→				})
   676→				continue
   677→			}
   678→			val, err := change.After.Decode(ty)
   679→			if err != nil {
   680→				diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   681→					Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   682→					Summary:  "Invalid resource instance data in plan",
   683→					Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("Instance %s data could not be decoded from the plan: %s.", instAddr, err),
   684→					Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   685→				})
   686→				continue
   687→			}
   688→
   689→			// If our provider schema contains sensitive values, mark those as sensitive
   690→			afterMarks := change.AfterValMarks
   691→			if schema.ContainsSensitive() {
   692→				afterMarks = append(afterMarks, schema.ValueMarks(val, nil)...)
   693→			}
   694→
   695→			instances[key] = val.MarkWithPaths(afterMarks)
   696→			continue
   697→		}
   698→
   699→		ios, err := is.Current.Decode(ty)
   700→		if err != nil {
   701→			// This shouldn't happen, since by the time we get here we
   702→			// should have upgraded the state data already.
   703→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   704→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   705→				Summary:  "Invalid resource instance data in state",
   706→				Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("Instance %s data could not be decoded from the state: %s.", instAddr, err),
   707→				Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   708→			})
   709→			continue
   710→		}
   711→
   712→		val := ios.Value
   713→
   714→		// If our schema contains sensitive values, mark those as sensitive.
   715→		// Since decoding the instance object can also apply sensitivity marks,
   716→		// we must remove and combine those before remarking to avoid a double-
   717→		// mark error.
   718→		if schema.ContainsSensitive() {
   719→			var marks []cty.PathValueMarks
   720→			val, marks = val.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
   721→			marks = append(marks, schema.ValueMarks(val, nil)...)
   722→			val = val.MarkWithPaths(marks)
   723→		}
   724→		instances[key] = val
   725→	}
   726→
   727→	// ret should be populated with a valid value in all cases below
   728→	var ret cty.Value
   729→
   730→	switch {
   731→	case config.Count != nil:
   732→		// figure out what the last index we have is
   733→		length := -1
   734→		for key := range instances {
   735→			intKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)
   736→			if !ok {
   737→				continue
   738→			}
   739→			if int(intKey) >= length {
   740→				length = int(intKey) + 1
   741→			}
   742→		}
   743→
   744→		if length > 0 {
   745→			vals := make([]cty.Value, length)
   746→			for key, instance := range instances {
   747→				intKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)
   748→				if !ok {
   749→					// old key from state, which isn't valid for evaluation
   750→					continue
   751→				}
   752→
   753→				vals[int(intKey)] = instance
   754→			}
   755→
   756→			// Insert unknown values where there are any missing instances
   757→			for i, v := range vals {
   758→				if v == cty.NilVal {
   759→					vals[i] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   760→				}
   761→			}
   762→			ret = cty.TupleVal(vals)
   763→		} else {
   764→			ret = cty.EmptyTupleVal
   765→		}
   766→
   767→	case config.ForEach != nil:
   768→		vals := make(map[string]cty.Value)
   769→		for key, instance := range instances {
   770→			strKey, ok := key.(addrs.StringKey)
   771→			if !ok {
   772→				// old key that is being dropped and not used for evaluation
   773→				continue
   774→			}
   775→			vals[string(strKey)] = instance
   776→		}
   777→
   778→		if len(vals) > 0 {
   779→			// We use an object rather than a map here because resource schemas
   780→			// may include dynamically-typed attributes, which will then cause
   781→			// each instance to potentially have a different runtime type even
   782→			// though they all conform to the static schema.
   783→			ret = cty.ObjectVal(vals)
   784→		} else {
   785→			ret = cty.EmptyObjectVal
   786→		}
   787→
   788→	default:
   789→		val, ok := instances[addrs.NoKey]
   790→		if !ok {
   791→			// if the instance is missing, insert an unknown value
   792→			val = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   793→		}
   794→
   795→		ret = val
   796→	}
   797→
   798→	return ret, diags
   799→}
   800→
   801→func (d *evaluationStateData) getResourceSchema(addr addrs.Resource, providerAddr addrs.Provider) *configschema.Block {
   802→	schema, _, err := d.Evaluator.Plugins.ResourceTypeSchema(providerAddr, addr.Mode, addr.Type)
   803→	if err != nil {
   804→		// We have plently other codepaths that will detect and report
   805→		// schema lookup errors before we'd reach this point, so we'll just
   806→		// treat a failure here the same as having no schema.
   807→		return nil
   808→	}
   809→	return schema
   810→}
   811→
   812→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetTerraformAttr(addr addrs.TerraformAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   813→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   814→
   815→	if d.Evaluator.Meta == nil || d.Evaluator.Meta.Env == "" {
   816→		// The absense of an "env" (really: workspace) name suggests that
   817→		// we're running in a non-workspace context, such as in a component
   818→		// of a stack. terraform.workspace -- and the terraform symbol in
   819→		// general -- is a legacy thing from workspaces mode that isn't
   820→		// carried forward to stacks, because stack configurations can instead
   821→		// vary their behavior based on input variables provided in the
   822→		// deployment configuration.
   823→		switch addr.Name {
   824→		case "workspace":
   825→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   826→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   827→				Summary:  `Invalid reference`,
   828→				Detail:   `The terraform.workspace attribute is only available for modules used in Terraform workspaces. Use input variables instead to create variations between different instances of this module.`,
   829→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   830→			})
   831→		default:
   832→			// A more generic error for any other attribute name, since no
   833→			// others are valid anyway but it would be confusing to mention
   834→			// terraform.workspace here.
   835→			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   836→				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   837→				Summary:  `Invalid reference`,
   838→				Detail:   `The "terraform" object is only available for modules used in Terraform workspaces.`,
   839→				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   840→			})
   841→		}
   842→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   843→	}
   844→
   845→	switch addr.Name {
   846→
   847→	case "workspace":
   848→		workspaceName := d.Evaluator.Meta.Env
   849→		return cty.StringVal(workspaceName), diags
   850→
   851→	case "env":
   852→		// Prior to Terraform 0.12 there was an attribute "env", which was
   853→		// an alias name for "workspace". This was deprecated and is now
   854→		// removed.
   855→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   856→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   857→			Summary:  `Invalid "terraform" attribute`,
   858→			Detail:   `The terraform.env attribute was deprecated in v0.10 and removed in v0.12. The "state environment" concept was renamed to "workspace" in v0.12, and so the workspace name can now be accessed using the terraform.workspace attribute.`,
   859→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   860→		})
   861→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   862→
   863→	default:
   864→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   865→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   866→			Summary:  `Invalid "terraform" attribute`,
   867→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "terraform" object does not have an attribute named %q. The only supported attribute is terraform.workspace, the name of the currently-selected workspace.`, addr.Name),
   868→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   869→		})
   870→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   871→	}
   872→}
   873→
   874→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetOutput(addr addrs.OutputValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   875→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   876→
   877→	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   878→	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   879→	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendentForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   880→	if moduleConfig == nil {
   881→		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   882→		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   883→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("output value read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   884→	}
   885→
   886→	config := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs[addr.Name]
   887→	if config == nil {
   888→		var suggestions []string
   889→		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Outputs {
   890→			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   891→		}
   892→		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   893→		if suggestion != "" {
   894→			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   895→		}
   896→
   897→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   898→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   899→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared output value`,
   900→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An output value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   901→			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   902→		})
   903→		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   904→	}
   905→
   906→	output := d.Evaluator.State.OutputValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   907→	if output == nil {
   908→		// Then the output itself returned null, so we'll package that up and
   909→		// pass it on.
   910→		output = &states.OutputValue{
   911→			Addr:      addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath),
   912→			Value:     cty.NilVal,
   913→			Sensitive: config.Sensitive,
   914→		}
   915→	} else if output.Value == cty.NilVal || output.Value.IsNull() {
   916→		// Then we did get a value but Terraform itself thought it was NilVal
   917→		// so we treat this as if the value isn't yet known.
   918→		output.Value = cty.DynamicVal
   919→	}
   920→
   921→	val := output.Value
   922→	if output.Sensitive {
   923→		val = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   924→	}
   925→
   926→	return val, diags
   927→}
   928→
   929→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetCheckBlock(addr addrs.Check, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   930→	// For now, check blocks don't contain any meaningful data and can only
   931→	// be referenced from the testing scope within an expect_failures attribute.
   932→	//
   933→	// We've added them into the scope explicitly since they are referencable,
   934→	// but we'll actually just return an error message saying they can't be
   935→	// referenced in this context.
   936→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   937→	diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   938→		Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   939→		Summary:  "Reference to \"check\" in invalid context",
   940→		Detail:   "The \"check\" object can only be referenced from an \"expect_failures\" attribute within a Terraform testing \"run\" block.",
   941→		Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   942→	})
   943→	return cty.NilVal, diags
   944→}
   945→
   946→func (d *evaluationStateData) GetRunBlock(run addrs.Run, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   947→	// We should not get here because any scope that has an [evaluationStateData]
   948→	// as its Data should have a reference parser that doesn't accept addrs.Run
   949→	// addresses.
   950→	panic("GetRunBlock called on non-test evaluation dataset")
   951→}
   952→
   953→// moduleDisplayAddr returns a string describing the given module instance
   954→// address that is appropriate for returning to users in situations where the
   955→// root module is possible. Specifically, it returns "the root module" if the
   956→// root module instance is given, or a string representation of the module
   957→// address otherwise.
   958→func moduleDisplayAddr(addr addrs.ModuleInstance) string {
   959→	switch {
   960→	case addr.IsRoot():
   961→		return "the root module"
   962→	default:
   963→		return addr.String()
   964→	}
   965→}
   966→

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→	"strings"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    12→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/checks"
    16→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    17→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema"
    18→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances"
    19→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking"
    20→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
    21→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange"
    22→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    23→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners"
    24→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    25→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    26→)
    27→
    28→// NodeAbstractResourceInstance represents a resource instance with no
    29→// associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally
    30→// contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many
    31→// instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using
    32→// the "count" or "for_each" arguments.
    33→type NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct {
    34→	NodeAbstractResource
    35→	Addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    36→
    37→	// These are set via the AttachState method.
    38→	instanceState *states.ResourceInstance
    39→
    40→	Dependencies []addrs.ConfigResource
    41→

725→func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) plan(
   726→	ctx EvalContext,
   727→	plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,
   728→	currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject,
   729→	createBeforeDestroy bool,
   730→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance,
   731→) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   732→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   733→	var keyData instances.RepetitionData
   734→
   735→	resource := n.Addr.Resource.Resource
   736→	provider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)
   737→	if err != nil {

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package states
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"sort"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    10→	ctyjson "github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty/json"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// ResourceInstanceObject is the local representation of a specific remote
    16→// object associated with a resource instance. In practice not all remote
    17→// objects are actually remote in the sense of being accessed over the network,
    18→// but this is the most common case.
    19→//
    20→// It is not valid to mutate a ResourceInstanceObject once it has been created.
    21→// Instead, create a new object and replace the existing one.
    22→type ResourceInstanceObject struct {
    23→	// Value is the object-typed value representing the remote object within
    24→	// Terraform.
    25→	Value cty.Value
    26→
    27→	// Private is an opaque value set by the provider when this object was
    28→	// last created or updated. Terraform Core does not use this value in
    29→	// any way and it is not exposed anywhere in the user interface, so
    30→	// a provider can use it for retaining any necessary private state.
    31→	Private []byte
    32→
    33→	// Status represents the "readiness" of the object as of the last time
    34→	// it was updated.
    35→	Status ObjectStatus
    36→
    37→	// Dependencies is a set of absolute address to other resources this
    38→	// instance dependeded on when it was applied. This is used to construct
    39→	// the dependency relationships for an object whose configuration is no
    40→	// longer available, such as if it has been removed from configuration
    41→	// altogether, or is now deposed.
    42→	Dependencies []addrs.ConfigResource
    43→
    44→	// CreateBeforeDestroy reflects the status of the lifecycle
    45→	// create_before_destroy option when this instance was last updated.
    46→	// Because create_before_destroy also effects the overall ordering of the
    47→	// destroy operations, we need to record the status to ensure a resource
    48→	// removed from the config will still be destroyed in the same manner.
    49→	CreateBeforeDestroy bool
    50→}
    51→
    52→// ObjectStatus represents the status of a RemoteObject.
    53→type ObjectStatus rune
    54→
    55→//go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type ObjectStatus
    56→
    57→const (
    58→	// ObjectReady is an object status for an object that is ready to use.
    59→	ObjectReady ObjectStatus = 'R'
    60→
    61→	// ObjectTainted is an object status representing an object that is in
    62→	// an unrecoverable bad state due to a partial failure during a create,
    63→	// update, or delete operation. Since it cannot be moved into the
    64→	// ObjectRead state, a tainted object must be replaced.
    65→	ObjectTainted ObjectStatus = 'T'
    66→
    67→	// ObjectPlanned is a special object status used only for the transient
    68→	// placeholder objects we place into state during the refresh and plan
    69→	// walks to stand in for objects that will be created during apply.
    70→	//
    71→	// Any object of this status must have a corresponding change recorded
    72→	// in the current plan, whose value must then be used in preference to
    73→	// the value stored in state when evaluating expressions. A planned
    74→	// object stored in state will be incomplete if any of its attributes are
    75→	// not yet known, and the plan must be consulted in order to "see" those
    76→	// unknown values, because the state is not able to represent them.
    77→	ObjectPlanned ObjectStatus = 'P'
    78→)
    79→
    80→// Encode marshals the value within the receiver to produce a
    81→// ResourceInstanceObjectSrc ready to be written to a state file.
    82→//
    83→// The given type must be the implied type of the resource type schema, and
    84→// the given value must conform to it. It is important to pass the schema
    85→// type and not the object's own type so that dynamically-typed attributes
    86→// will be stored correctly. The caller must also provide the version number
    87→// of the schema that the given type was derived from, which will be recorded
    88→// in the source object so it can be used to detect when schema migration is
    89→// required on read.
    90→//
    91→// The returned object may share internal references with the receiver and
    92→// so the caller must not mutate the receiver any further once once this
    93→// method is called.
    94→func (o *ResourceInstanceObject) Encode(ty cty.Type, schemaVersion uint64) (*ResourceInstanceObjectSrc, error) {
    95→	// If it contains marks, remove these marks before traversing the
    96→	// structure with UnknownAsNull, and save the PathValueMarks
    97→	// so we can save them in state.
    98→	val, pvm := o.Value.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
    99→
   100→	// Our state serialization can't represent unknown values, so we convert
   101→	// them to nulls here. This is lossy, but nobody should be writing unknown
   102→	// values here and expecting to get them out again later.
   103→	//
   104→	// We get unknown values here while we're building out a "planned state"
   105→	// during the plan phase, but the value stored in the plan takes precedence
   106→	// for expression evaluation. The apply step should never produce unknown
   107→	// values, but if it does it's the responsibility of the caller to detect
   108→	// and raise an error about that.
   109→	val = cty.UnknownAsNull(val)
   110→
   111→	src, err := ctyjson.Marshal(val, ty)
   112→	if err != nil {
   113→		return nil, err
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	// Dependencies are collected and merged in an unordered format (using map
   117→	// keys as a set), then later changed to a slice (in random ordering) to be
   118→	// stored in state as an array. To avoid pointless thrashing of state in
   119→	// refresh-only runs, we can either override comparison of dependency lists
   120→	// (more desirable, but tricky for Reasons) or just sort when encoding.
   121→	// Encoding of instances can happen concurrently, so we must copy the
   122→	// dependencies to avoid mutating what may be a shared array of values.
   123→	dependencies := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(o.Dependencies))
   124→	copy(dependencies, o.Dependencies)
   125→
   126→	sort.Slice(dependencies, func(i, j int) bool { return dependencies[i].String() < dependencies[j].String() })
   127→
   128→	return &ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{
   129→		SchemaVersion:       schemaVersion,
   130→		AttrsJSON:           src,
   131→		AttrSensitivePaths:  pvm,
   132→		Private:             o.Private,
   133→		Status:              o.Status,
   134→		Dependencies:        dependencies,
   135→		CreateBeforeDestroy: o.CreateBeforeDestroy,
   136→	}, nil
   137→}
   138→
   139→// AsTainted returns a deep copy of the receiver with the status updated to
   140→// ObjectTainted.
   141→func (o *ResourceInstanceObject) AsTainted() *ResourceInstanceObject {
   142→	if o == nil {
   143→		// A nil object can't be tainted, but we'll allow this anyway to
   144→		// avoid a crash, since we presumably intend to eventually record
   145→		// the object has having been deleted anyway.
   146→		return nil
   147→	}
   148→	ret := o.DeepCopy()
   149→	ret.Status = ObjectTainted
   150→	return ret
   151→}
   152→

    17→// calling method Decode with the implied type of the latest schema.

    20→	// was current when either AttrsJSON or AttrsFlat was encoded. Migration
    21→	// steps are required if this is less than the current version number
    22→	// reported by the corresponding provider.

    24→
    25→	// AttrsJSON is a JSON-encoded representation of the object attributes,
    26→	// encoding the value (of the object type implied by the associated resource
    27→	// type schema) that represents this remote object in Terraform Language
    28→	// expressions, and is compared with configuration when producing a diff.
    29→	//
    30→	// This is retained in JSON format here because it may require preprocessing
    31→	// before decoding if, for example, the stored attributes are for an older
    32→	// schema version which the provider must upgrade before use. If the
    33→	// version is current, it is valid to simply decode this using the
    34→	// type implied by the current schema, without the need for the provider
    35→	// to perform an upgrade first.

    38→	// always be conformant to the current schema version and the current
    39→	// schema version should be recorded in the SchemaVersion field.
    40→	AttrsJSON []byte
    41→
    42→	// AttrsFlat is a legacy form of attributes used in older state file
    43→	// formats, and in the new state format for objects that haven't yet been
    44→	// upgraded. This attribute is mutually exclusive with Attrs: for any
    45→	// ResourceInstanceObject, only one of these attributes may be populated
    46→	// and the other must be nil.

    49→	// Attrs at the earliest opportunity, since this legacy flatmap-based
    50→	// format will be phased out over time. AttrsFlat should not be used when
    51→	// writing new or updated objects to state; instead, callers must follow
    52→	// the recommendations in the AttrsJSON documentation above.

    54→

    56→	// state, or to save as sensitive paths when saving state

    71→// exactly equals the version number of the schema whose implied type is being

    75→// so the caller must not mutate the receiver any further once once this
    76→// method is called.

    82→		val, err = hcl2shim.HCL2ValueFromFlatmap(os.AttrsFlat, ty)

    98→		Value:               val,
    99→		Status:              os.Status,
   100→		Dependencies:        os.Dependencies,

   106→// CompleteUpgrade creates a new ResourceInstanceObjectSrc by copying the
   107→// metadata from the receiver and writing in the given new schema version
   108→// and attribute value that are presumed to have resulted from upgrading

   115→	// avoiding a decode/re-encode cycle because we don't have type info

    37→	// When writing a ResourceInstanceObject into the state, AttrsJSON should
    38→	// always be conformant to the current schema version and the current
    39→	// schema version should be recorded in the SchemaVersion field.
    40→	AttrsJSON []byte
    41→
    42→	// AttrsFlat is a legacy form of attributes used in older state file
    43→	// formats, and in the new state format for objects that haven't yet been
    44→	// upgraded. This attribute is mutually exclusive with Attrs: for any
    45→	// ResourceInstanceObject, only one of these attributes may be populated
    46→	// and the other must be nil.

    49→	// Attrs at the earliest opportunity, since this legacy flatmap-based
    50→	// format will be phased out over time. AttrsFlat should not be used when
    51→	// writing new or updated objects to state; instead, callers must follow
    52→	// the recommendations in the AttrsJSON documentation above.
    53→	AttrsFlat map[string]string
    54→
    55→	// AttrSensitivePaths is an array of paths to mark as sensitive coming out of
    56→	// state, or to save as sensitive paths when saving state
    57→	AttrSensitivePaths []cty.PathValueMarks
    58→
    59→	// These fields all correspond to the fields of the same name on
    60→	// ResourceInstanceObject.
    61→	Private             []byte
    62→	Status              ObjectStatus
    63→	Dependencies        []addrs.ConfigResource
    64→	CreateBeforeDestroy bool
    65→}
    66→
    67→// Decode unmarshals the raw representation of the object attributes. Pass the
    68→// implied type of the corresponding resource type schema for correct operation.
    69→//
    70→// Before calling Decode, the caller must check that the SchemaVersion field
    71→// exactly equals the version number of the schema whose implied type is being
    72→// passed, or else the result is undefined.
    73→//
    74→// The returned object may share internal references with the receiver and
    75→// so the caller must not mutate the receiver any further once once this
    76→// method is called.
    77→func (os *ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) Decode(ty cty.Type) (*ResourceInstanceObject, error) {
    78→	var val cty.Value
    79→	var err error
    80→	if os.AttrsFlat != nil {
    81→		// Legacy mode. We'll do our best to unpick this from the flatmap.
    82→		val, err = hcl2shim.HCL2ValueFromFlatmap(os.AttrsFlat, ty)
    83→		if err != nil {
    84→			return nil, err
    85→		}
    86→	} else {
    87→		val, err = ctyjson.Unmarshal(os.AttrsJSON, ty)
    88→		// Mark the value with paths if applicable
    89→		if os.AttrSensitivePaths != nil {
    90→			val = val.MarkWithPaths(os.AttrSensitivePaths)
    91→		}
    92→		if err != nil {
    93→			return nil, err
    94→		}
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	return &ResourceInstanceObject{
    98→		Value:               val,
    99→		Status:              os.Status,
   100→		Dependencies:        os.Dependencies,
   101→		Private:             os.Private,
   102→		CreateBeforeDestroy: os.CreateBeforeDestroy,
   103→	}, nil
   104→}
   105→
   106→// CompleteUpgrade creates a new ResourceInstanceObjectSrc by copying the
   107→// metadata from the receiver and writing in the given new schema version
   108→// and attribute value that are presumed to have resulted from upgrading
   109→// from an older schema version.

   113→

   115→	// avoiding a decode/re-encode cycle because we don't have type info
   116→	// available for the "old" attributes.
   117→	newAttrs = cty.UnknownAsNull(newAttrs)
   118→	src, err := ctyjson.Marshal(newAttrs, newType)
   119→	if err != nil {
   120→		return nil, err
   121→	}
   122→
   123→	new.AttrsJSON = src
   124→	new.SchemaVersion = newSchemaVersion
   125→	return new, nil
   126→}

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package jsonstate
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"encoding/json"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→	"sort"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    12→	ctyjson "github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty/json"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/command/jsonchecks"
    16→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks"
    17→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    18→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states/statefile"
    19→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/terraform"
    20→)
    21→
    22→const (
    23→	// FormatVersion represents the version of the json format and will be
    24→	// incremented for any change to this format that requires changes to a
    25→	// consuming parser.
    26→	FormatVersion = "1.0"
    27→
    28→	ManagedResourceMode = "managed"
    29→	DataResourceMode    = "data"
    30→)
    31→
    32→// state is the top-level representation of the json format of a terraform
    33→// state.
    34→type state struct {
    35→	FormatVersion    string          `json:"format_version,omitempty"`
    36→	TerraformVersion string          `json:"terraform_version,omitempty"`
    37→	Values           *stateValues    `json:"values,omitempty"`
    38→	Checks           json.RawMessage `json:"checks,omitempty"`
    39→}
    40→
    41→// stateValues is the common representation of resolved values for both the prior
    42→// state (which is always complete) and the planned new state.
    43→type stateValues struct {
    44→	Outputs    map[string]Output `json:"outputs,omitempty"`
    45→	RootModule Module            `json:"root_module,omitempty"`
    46→}
    47→
    48→type Output struct {
    49→	Sensitive bool            `json:"sensitive"`
    50→	Value     json.RawMessage `json:"value,omitempty"`
    51→	Type      json.RawMessage `json:"type,omitempty"`
    52→}
    53→
    54→// Module is the representation of a module in state. This can be the root module
    55→// or a child module
    56→type Module struct {
    57→	// Resources are sorted in a user-friendly order that is undefined at this
    58→	// time, but consistent.
    59→	Resources []Resource `json:"resources,omitempty"`
    60→
    61→	// Address is the absolute module address, omitted for the root module
    62→	Address string `json:"address,omitempty"`
    63→
    64→	// Each module object can optionally have its own nested "child_modules",
    65→	// recursively describing the full module tree.
    66→	ChildModules []Module `json:"child_modules,omitempty"`
    67→}
    68→
    69→// Resource is the representation of a resource in the state.
    70→type Resource struct {
    71→	// Address is the absolute resource address
    72→	Address string `json:"address,omitempty"`
    73→
    74→	// Mode can be "managed" or "data"
    75→	Mode string `json:"mode,omitempty"`
    76→
    77→	Type string `json:"type,omitempty"`
    78→	Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`
    79→
    80→	// Index is omitted for a resource not using `count` or `for_each`.
    81→	Index json.RawMessage `json:"index,omitempty"`
    82→
    83→	// ProviderName allows the property "type" to be interpreted unambiguously
    84→	// in the unusual situation where a provider offers a resource type whose
    85→	// name does not start with its own name, such as the "googlebeta" provider
    86→	// offering "google_compute_instance".
    87→	ProviderName string `json:"provider_name"`
    88→
    89→	// SchemaVersion indicates which version of the resource type schema the
    90→	// "values" property conforms to.
    91→	SchemaVersion uint64 `json:"schema_version"`
    92→
    93→	// AttributeValues is the JSON representation of the attribute values of the
    94→	// resource, whose structure depends on the resource type schema. Any
    95→	// unknown values are omitted or set to null, making them indistinguishable
    96→	// from absent values.
    97→	AttributeValues AttributeValues `json:"values,omitempty"`
    98→
    99→	// SensitiveValues is similar to AttributeValues, but with all sensitive
   100→	// values replaced with true, and all non-sensitive leaf values omitted.
   101→	SensitiveValues json.RawMessage `json:"sensitive_values,omitempty"`
   102→
   103→	// DependsOn contains a list of the resource's dependencies. The entries are
   104→	// addresses relative to the containing module.
   105→	DependsOn []string `json:"depends_on,omitempty"`
   106→
   107→	// Tainted is true if the resource is tainted in terraform state.
   108→	Tainted bool `json:"tainted,omitempty"`
   109→
   110→	// Deposed is set if the resource is deposed in terraform state.
   111→	DeposedKey string `json:"deposed_key,omitempty"`
   112→}
   113→
   114→// AttributeValues is the JSON representation of the attribute values of the
   115→// resource, whose structure depends on the resource type schema.
   116→type AttributeValues map[string]json.RawMessage
   117→
   118→func marshalAttributeValues(value cty.Value) AttributeValues {
   119→	// unmark our value to show all values
   120→	value, _ = value.UnmarkDeep()
   121→
   122→	if value == cty.NilVal || value.IsNull() {
   123→		return nil
   124→	}
   125→
   126→	ret := make(AttributeValues)
   127→
   128→	it := value.ElementIterator()
   129→	for it.Next() {
   130→		k, v := it.Element()
   131→		vJSON, _ := ctyjson.Marshal(v, v.Type())
   132→		ret[k.AsString()] = json.RawMessage(vJSON)
   133→	}
   134→	return ret
   135→}
   136→
   137→// newState() returns a minimally-initialized state
   138→func newState() *state {
   139→	return &state{
   140→		FormatVersion: FormatVersion,
   141→	}
   142→}
   143→
   144→// MarshalForRenderer returns the pre-json encoding changes of the state, in a
   145→// format available to the structured renderer.
   146→func MarshalForRenderer(sf *statefile.File, schemas *terraform.Schemas) (Module, map[string]Output, error) {
   147→	if sf.State.Modules == nil {
   148→		// Empty state case.
   149→		return Module{}, nil, nil
   150→	}

Any\n\t// unknown values are omitted or set to null, making them indistinguishable\n\t// from absent values.\n\tAttributeValues AttributeValues `json:\"values,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// SensitiveValues is similar to AttributeValues, but with all sensitive\n\t// values replaced with true, and all non-sensitive leaf values omitted.\n\tSensitiveValues json.RawMessage `json:\"sensitive_values,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// DependsOn contains a list of the resource's dependencies. The entries are\n\t// addresses relative to the containing module.\n\tDependsOn []string `json:\"depends_on,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Tainted is true if the resource is tainted in terraform state.\n\tTainted bool `json:\"tainted,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Deposed is set if the resource is deposed in terraform state.\n\tDeposedKey string `json:\"deposed_key,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// AttributeValues is the JSON representation of the attribute values of the\n// resource, whose structure depends on the resource type schema.\ntype AttributeValues map[string]json.RawMessage\n\nfunc marshalAttributeValues(value cty.Value) AttributeValues {\n\t// unmark our value to show all values\n\tvalue, _ = value.UnmarkDeep()\n\n\tif value == cty.NilVal || value.IsNull() {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tret := make(AttributeValues)\n\n\tit := value.ElementIterator()\n\tfor it.Next() {\n\t\tk, v := it.Element()\n\t\tvJSON, _ := ctyjson.Marshal(v, v.Type())\n\t\tret[k.AsString()] = json.RawMessage(vJSON)\n\t}\n\treturn ret\n}\n\n// newState() returns a minimally-initialized state\nfunc newState() *state {\n\treturn &state{\n\t\tFormatVersion: FormatVersion,\n\t}\n}\n\n// MarshalForRenderer returns the pre-json encoding changes of the state, in a\n// format available to the structured renderer.\nfunc MarshalForRenderer(sf *statefile.File, schemas *terraform.Schemas) (Module, map[string]Output, error) {\n\tif sf.State.Modules == nil {\n\t\t// Empty state case.\n\t\treturn Module{}, nil, nil\n\t}", "numLines": 150, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 555}}

   118→func marshalAttributeValues(value cty.Value) AttributeValues {
   119→	// unmark our value to show all values
   120→	value, _ = value.UnmarkDeep()
   121→

   126→	ret := make(AttributeValues)

   146→func MarshalForRenderer(sf *statefile.File, schemas *terraform.Schemas) (Module, map[string]Output, error) {

  1113→	if action.IsReplace() {
  1114→		// In this strange situation we want to produce a change object that
  1115→		// shows our real prior object but has a _new_ object that is built
  1116→		// from a null prior object, since we're going to delete the one
  1117→		// that has all the computed values on it.
  1118→		//
  1119→		// Therefore we'll ask the provider to plan again here, giving it
  1120→		// a null object for the prior, and then we'll meld that with the
  1121→		// _actual_ prior state to produce a correctly-shaped replace change.
  1122→		// The resulting change should show any computed attributes changing
  1123→		// from known prior values to unknown values, unless the provider is
  1124→		// able to predict new values for any of these computed attributes.
  1125→		nullPriorVal := cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())
  1126→
  1127→		// Since there is no prior state to compare after replacement, we need
  1128→		// a new unmarked config from our original with no ignored values.
  1129→		unmarkedConfigVal := origConfigVal
  1130→		if origConfigVal.ContainsMarked() {
  1131→			unmarkedConfigVal, _ = origConfigVal.UnmarkDeep()
  1132→		}
  1133→
  1134→		// create a new proposed value from the null state and the config
  1135→		proposedNewVal = objchange.ProposedNew(schema, nullPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)
  1136→
  1137→		if n.override != nil {
  1138→			// In this case, we are always creating the resource so we don't
  1139→			// do any validation, and just call out to the mocking library.
  1140→			override, overrideDiags := mocking.PlanComputedValuesForResource(proposedNewVal, schema)
  1141→			resp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{
  1142→				PlannedState: override,
  1143→				Diagnostics:  overrideDiags,
  1144→			}
  1145→		} else {
  1146→			resp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{
  1147→				TypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,
  1148→				Config:           unmarkedConfigVal,
  1149→				PriorState:       nullPriorVal,
  1150→				ProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,
  1151→				PriorPrivate:     plannedPrivate,
  1152→				ProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,
  1153→			})

  1155→		// We need to tread carefully here, since if there are any warnings
  1156→		// in here they probably also came out of our previous call to
  1157→		// PlanResourceChange above, and so we don't want to repeat them.
  1158→		// Consequently, we break from the usual pattern here and only
  1159→		// append these new diagnostics if there's at least one error inside.
  1160→		if resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {
  1161→			diags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))
  1162→			return nil, nil, keyData, diags

  1164→		plannedNewVal = resp.PlannedState

  1171→		for _, err := range plannedNewVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {

  1178→				),

  1183→		}
  1184→	}

  1186→	// If our prior value was tainted then we actually want this to appear

  1189→	if action == plans.Create && !priorValTainted.IsNull() {

  1199→	// If we plan to write or delete sensitive paths from state,

   309→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics

   318→func (ctx *BuiltinEvalContext) EvaluateExpr(expr hcl.Expression, wantType cty.Type, self addrs.Referenceable) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   319→	scope := ctx.EvaluationScope(self, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)
   320→	return scope.EvalExpr(expr, wantType)
   321→}
   322→
   323→func (ctx *BuiltinEvalContext) EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr hcl.Expression, repData instances.RepetitionData) (*addrs.Reference, bool, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   324→
   325→	// get the reference to lookup changes in the plan
   326→	ref, diags := evalReplaceTriggeredByExpr(expr, repData)
   327→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   328→		return nil, false, diags
   329→	}
   330→
   331→	var changes []*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc
   332→	// store the address once we get it for validation
   333→	var resourceAddr addrs.Resource
   334→
   335→	// The reference is either a resource or resource instance
   336→	switch sub := ref.Subject.(type) {
   337→	case addrs.Resource:
   338→		resourceAddr = sub
   339→		rc := sub.Absolute(ctx.Path())
   340→		changes = ctx.Changes().GetChangesForAbsResource(rc)
   341→	case addrs.ResourceInstance:
   342→		resourceAddr = sub.ContainingResource()
   343→		rc := sub.Absolute(ctx.Path())
   344→		change := ctx.Changes().GetResourceInstanceChange(rc, addrs.NotDeposed)
   345→		if change != nil {
   346→			// we'll generate an error below if there was no change
   347→			changes = append(changes, change)
   348→		}
   349→	}
   350→
   351→	// Do some validation to make sure we are expecting a change at all
   352→	cfg := ctx.Evaluator.Config.Descendent(ctx.Path().Module())
   353→	resCfg := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(resourceAddr)
   354→	if resCfg == nil {
   355→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   356→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   357→			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared resource`,
   358→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A resource %s has not been declared in %s`, ref.Subject, moduleDisplayAddr(ctx.Path())),
   359→			Subject:  expr.Range().Ptr(),
   360→		})
   361→		return nil, false, diags
   362→	}
   363→
   364→	if len(changes) == 0 {
   365→		// If the resource is valid there should always be at least one change.
   366→		diags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf("no change found for %s in %s", ref.Subject, moduleDisplayAddr(ctx.Path())))
   367→		return nil, false, diags
   368→	}
   369→
   370→	// If we don't have a traversal beyond the resource, then we can just look
   371→	// for any change.
   372→	if len(ref.Remaining) == 0 {
   373→		for _, c := range changes {
   374→			switch c.ChangeSrc.Action {
   375→			// Only immediate changes to the resource will trigger replacement.
   376→			case plans.Update, plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:
   377→				return ref, true, diags
   378→			}
   379→		}
   380→
   381→		// no change triggered
   382→		return nil, false, diags
   383→	}
   384→
   385→	// This must be an instances to have a remaining traversal, which means a
   386→	// single change.
   387→	change := changes[0]

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package objchange
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"errors"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// ProposedNew constructs a proposed new object value by combining the
    16→// computed attribute values from "prior" with the configured attribute values
    17→// from "config".
    18→//
    19→// Both value must conform to the given schema's implied type, or this function
    20→// will panic.
    21→//
    22→// The prior value must be wholly known, but the config value may be unknown
    23→// or have nested unknown values.
    24→//
    25→// The merging of the two objects includes the attributes of any nested blocks,
    26→// which will be correlated in a manner appropriate for their nesting mode.
    27→// Note in particular that the correlation for blocks backed by sets is a
    28→// heuristic based on matching non-computed attribute values and so it may
    29→// produce strange results with more "extreme" cases, such as a nested set
    30→// block where _all_ attributes are computed.
    31→func ProposedNew(schema *configschema.Block, prior, config cty.Value) cty.Value {
    32→	// If the config and prior are both null, return early here before
    33→	// populating the prior block. The prevents non-null blocks from appearing
    34→	// the proposed state value.
    35→	if config.IsNull() && prior.IsNull() {
    36→		return prior
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	if prior.IsNull() {
    40→		// In this case, we will construct a synthetic prior value that is
    41→		// similar to the result of decoding an empty configuration block,
    42→		// which simplifies our handling of the top-level attributes/blocks
    43→		// below by giving us one non-null level of object to pull values from.
    44→		//
    45→		// "All attributes null" happens to be the definition of EmptyValue for
    46→		// a Block, so we can just delegate to that
    47→		prior = schema.EmptyValue()
    48→	}
    49→	return proposedNew(schema, prior, config)
    50→}
    51→
    52→// PlannedDataResourceObject is similar to proposedNewBlock but tailored for
    53→// planning data resources in particular. Specifically, it replaces the values
    54→// of any Computed attributes not set in the configuration with an unknown
    55→// value, which serves as a placeholder for a value to be filled in by the
    56→// provider when the data resource is finally read.
    57→//
    58→// Data resources are different because the planning of them is handled
    59→// entirely within Terraform Core and not subject to customization by the
    60→// provider. This function is, in effect, producing an equivalent result to
    61→// passing the proposedNewBlock result into a provider's PlanResourceChange
    62→// function, assuming a fixed implementation of PlanResourceChange that just
    63→// fills in unknown values as needed.
    64→func PlannedDataResourceObject(schema *configschema.Block, config cty.Value) cty.Value {
    65→	// Our trick here is to run the proposedNewBlock logic with an
    66→	// entirely-unknown prior value. Because of cty's unknown short-circuit
    67→	// behavior, any operation on prior returns another unknown, and so
    68→	// unknown values propagate into all of the parts of the resulting value
    69→	// that would normally be filled in by preserving the prior state.
    70→	prior := cty.UnknownVal(schema.ImpliedType())
    71→	return proposedNew(schema, prior, config)
    72→}
    73→
    74→func proposedNew(schema *configschema.Block, prior, config cty.Value) cty.Value {
    75→	if config.IsNull() || !config.IsKnown() {
    76→		// A block config should never be null at this point. The only nullable
    77→		// block type is NestingSingle, which will return early before coming
    78→		// back here. We'll allow the null here anyway to free callers from
    79→		// needing to specifically check for these cases, and any mismatch will
    80→		// be caught in validation, so just take the prior value rather than
    81→		// the invalid null.
    82→		return prior
    83→	}
    84→
    85→	if (!prior.Type().IsObjectType()) || (!config.Type().IsObjectType()) {
    86→		panic("ProposedNew only supports object-typed values")
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	// From this point onwards, we can assume that both values are non-null
    90→	// object types, and that the config value itself is known (though it
    91→	// may contain nested values that are unknown.)
    92→	newAttrs := proposedNewAttributes(schema.Attributes, prior, config)
    93→
    94→	// Merging nested blocks is a little more complex, since we need to
    95→	// correlate blocks between both objects and then recursively propose
    96→	// a new object for each. The correlation logic depends on the nesting
    97→	// mode for each block type.
    98→	for name, blockType := range schema.BlockTypes {
    99→		priorV := prior.GetAttr(name)
   100→		configV := config.GetAttr(name)
   101→		newAttrs[name] = proposedNewNestedBlock(blockType, priorV, configV)
   102→	}
   103→
   104→	return cty.ObjectVal(newAttrs)
   105→}
   106→
   107→// proposedNewBlockOrObject dispatched the schema to either ProposedNew or
   108→// proposedNewObjectAttributes depending on the given type.
   109→func proposedNewBlockOrObject(schema nestedSchema, prior, config cty.Value) cty.Value {
   110→	switch schema := schema.(type) {
   111→	case *configschema.Block:
   112→		return ProposedNew(schema, prior, config)
   113→	case *configschema.Object:
   114→		return proposedNewObjectAttributes(schema, prior, config)
   115→	default:
   116→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unexpected schema type %T", schema))
   117→	}
   118→}
   119→
   120→func proposedNewNestedBlock(schema *configschema.NestedBlock, prior, config cty.Value) cty.Value {
